headline: Cyril Imhof's cycle centers on Claude reaching enterprise collaboration platforms and the cost pressures pushing companies to build their own AI stacks.
body: Imhof repeatedly frames Claude's integration into Microsoft Teams and Slack as an enterprise adoption play, questioning whether Microsoft's EU position and its own Copilot limit third-party partnerships and whether Anthropic's direct Teams push can beat Microsoft's broader agent-market strategy [1][4][5][7]. He centers Model Context Protocols as the mechanism for external agent access, positioning them as a client retention and licensing tactic [2][3]. On economics, he argues rising API prices are driving firms toward internal SaaS built on Claude Artifacts and toward cheaper Chinese models for proprietary CRMs, citing cost and data sovereignty advantages [6][11][12][13]. He also claims the gap between open-source and frontier models is narrowing from annual to monthly cycles, and that Claude Tag accounts for 60% of Anthropic's internal interaction [8][9][10].
